primary_accounting_issue: Whether an intragroup dividend receivable (a monetary item) can be designated as a hedged item for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements under IFRS hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs which items qualify as eligible hedged items, including the requirement that hedged items must be with external parties (para 6.3.5) and the exception for intragroup monetary items (para 6.3.6)
Specifically addresses hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations and clarifies eligible hedged items and hedging instruments in group structures

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies that intragroup monetary assets cannot be eliminated against intragroup liabilities without recognizing currency fluctuations, supporting the existence of an exposure to be hedged

peripheral_authority.reason: Deals with disclosure requirements for hedge accounting and credit risk, not with the eligibility of hedged items
Addresses presentation of financial instruments as liabilities or equity, not hedge accounting eligibility
Disclosures about interests in subsidiaries, not directly relevant to hedge accounting treatment
Cash flow statement disclosure requirements for financing activities, not relevant to hedge accounting eligibility

treatment_families.family: Hedged item eligibility for intragroup monetary items
Recognition of foreign exchange gains/losses on intragroup items

approaches.label: Eligible hedged item - external party requirement
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 para 6.3.5 generally requires hedged items to involve external parties, which would exclude intragroup dividend receivables unless an exception applies

approaches.label: Net investment hedge for intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 and IFRS 9 para 6.3.6 provide exceptions permitting foreign currency risk of certain intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items in specific circumstances

approaches.label: Foreign currency transaction recognition without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 requires recognition of exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in profit or loss even when not designated as hedged items
